title: Fix /give command injection — sanitize block names in creative provisioning
description:
SECURITY FINDING (Council Seat 7): ProvisionGoalIfCreativeAsync builds `/give @p {block} {need}` where `block` comes from Blueprint.Materials[].Block — a string parsed from wiki page frontmatter with NO validation. Blueprint pages can be created/modified via CreatePageTool (MemorySmith wiki), accepting user/LLM-provided content. The adapter's creativeProvider.js also falls back to /give via bot.chat() — same vector.

Fix:
1. Add an allowlist of valid Minecraft item IDs before /give dispatch. Reject unknown block names.
2. Sanitize `block` to alphanumeric + underscores only. Reject spaces, semicolons, or shell metacharacters.
3. Move creative provisioning to adapter's creative.setInventorySlot() exclusively where possible — never fall back to /give chat on servers without OP.
4. Add a server-side CommandExecutionEnabled check in the "command" intent handler.

Referenced in: Data/Pages/council/2026-06-30-external-audits-council.md

comments.author: anonymous
comments.body: Created by SteveBot council review. Security finding — /give command injection via unsanitized block names from blueprints. P0 fix required. Block names from Blueprint.Materials[].Block flow directly into `/give @p {block} {need}` without validation. See council report Data/Pages/council/2026-06-30-external-audits-council.md for details.
